entries.value: Target control effectiveness rate is >95%. Below 90% triggers a compliance program review. Test all critical controls quarterly, standard controls annually. Ineffective controls require immediate remediation plans.
Target audit finding closure: critical <14 days, high <30 days, medium <60 days, low <90 days. Track average closure time by severity. Overdue findings require executive escalation.

All policies must be reviewed at least annually. Critical policies (information security, data privacy) require semi-annual review. Policy review completion rate target is 100% -- no exceptions for overdue reviews.
Target initial assessment of regulatory changes within 14 days of identification. Critical changes require 48-hour triage. Full implementation plans due within 30 days of assessment completion.
Target overall compliance score is >90%. Calculated as (effective controls / total applicable controls) * 100. Below 80% requires board-level reporting and corrective action plan.

Open findings count should trend downward quarter-over-quarter. More than 10 open high/critical findings indicates systemic control weaknesses. Zero open critical findings is a prerequisite for SOC 2 Type II.

SOC 2 Trust Service Criteria: Security (CC), Availability (A), Processing Integrity (PI), Confidentiality (C), Privacy (P). Security is mandatory. Others are optional but recommended for enterprise customers.
Risk acceptance requires VP-level approval for medium risks, CISO approval for high risks, and board approval for critical risks. All accepted risks must be re-evaluated quarterly.

Evidence for controls must be automated where possible. Manual evidence collection is error-prone and expensive. Target 80% automated evidence collection. Store evidence centrally with tamper-proof audit trails.
Shift from point-in-time audits to continuous compliance monitoring. Implement automated control testing, real-time alerting on control failures, and dashboard visibility for compliance leadership.
